title: sorbus torminalis var. mollis
intro: Sorbus torminalis var. mollis, also known as the whitebeam tree, is a deciduous tree species native to Europe, characterized by its white or pale yellow flowers and red berries. The medicinal properties of Sorbus torminalis var. mollis lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches and arthritis, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of Sorbus torminalis var. mollis typically involve drying the bark to create a tea or infusion, which can be used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
